mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% in a tightening cycle signals restrictive monetary conditions that increase discount rates on equity valuations and reduce corporate borrowing capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.25 percentage points over preceding 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 689443 million dollars as of May 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances following RSAFS declines > 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth rate decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 52% of instances when INDPRO growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously, signaling synchronized contraction across residential and consumer demand channels that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint signal is stronger than either component alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 1.0% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1427 thousand units as of May 2008
fact:RSAFS at 689443 million dollars as of May 2008
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following joint HOUST and RSAFS declines are positive in > 65% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50 thousand persons month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.15 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153743 thousand as of June 2008
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 62% of instances when both PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises occur, or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.12) in a regression model controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS > 3.5%), inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates month-over-month above 0.3%, signaling stagflationary pressures that compress real equity returns and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when both FEDFUNDS > 3.5% and CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3%, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime (GDPC1 growth decelerating), housing starts (HOUST) decline > 8% month-over-month, signaling imminent demand collapse that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional and inactive in early-cycle expansions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 8% month-over-month in late-cycle regime
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1427 thousand units as of May 2008
fact:GDPC1 at 20054.663 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2008
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of instances following HOUST declines > 8% in late-cycle regimes, or the mechanism produces negative Rank IC when applied to early-cycle expansion periods (p > 0.20).
